judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit every expectation with accurate, evidence-grounded findings and are essentially comparable in quality.

report_1.evidence: **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** This is false. `app/security/config.py:3-4` states explicitly: `# Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic.` / `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`. ... **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** This is not implemented. `app/routers/visits.py:7-11` shows the `/visits` route returns an empty list, with a bare `# TODO: pdf export` comment and no export logic, no PDF generation code anywhere in the repo ... **Must be removed**

report_2.evidence: **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF"** (line 24): False as written. `app/routers/visits.py` contains only a stub `GET /visits` that returns an empty list, with a literal `# TODO: pdf export` comment ... This must be removed before sending. **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default"** (line 30): False. `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` ... must be corrected or removed.

report_1.evidence: Per `captures.json`, `after_scheduling.png` was actually captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not `/schedule`. I opened the image directly and it renders the text "BILLING / INVOICES" ... the "after" half of this comparison is a screenshot of the billing/invoices page mislabeled as the new scheduling calendar

report_2.evidence: `after_scheduling.png` renders the text "BILLING / INVOICES" — a completely unrelated screen. This is confirmed by `docs/release/captures.json` ... `after_scheduling.png` → `/billing/invoices`. The "after" image is not a redesigned calendar at all; it's a screenshot of the billing/invoices surface.

report_1.evidence: **`after_export.png` does not exist.** ... Sending the doc as-is means a broken image link in the "Export your visit history" section ... **`unused_capture_07.png`** (renders "PATIENT LIST") is present on disk but not referenced anywhere in the draft

report_2.evidence: That file **does not exist** in `docs/release/img/` ... This is a broken image reference ... `docs/release/img/unused_capture_07.png` exists on disk (renders "PATIENT LIST") but is **never referenced** anywhere in `highlights-v4.md`.

report_1.evidence: the closing line **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** This is an internal drafting note, not customer-facing content ... It should be stripped before sending regardless of the other fixes.

report_2.evidence: The trailing line **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** (line 33) is an internal drafting/attribution note, not customer-facing content ... should be stripped
